metadata.title: RAGfly — RAG infrastructure for your agents
metadata.description: RAG at scale so any agent can converse with thousands of private documents — multi-tenant, isolated per client, and without the data ever leaving your network. Connect a directory via MCP, REST or CLI.

hero.tagline: Any agent conversing with thousands of private documents: at scale, isolated per client, inside your network. Connect a directory and your agent answers with citations — without building or maintaining a RAG pipeline.

construyendo.descripcion: Consume it from wherever you work — MCP, REST or CLI. The document context layer comes solved; you build the agent logic.
construyendo.cardATitulo: I'm building an agent for one client
construyendo.cardADesc: You have a large corpus and don't want to own the RAG infrastructure. Point RAGfly at the directory and build the agent logic, not the pipeline.
construyendo.cardBTitulo: I deploy agents for many clients
construyendo.cardBDesc: You serve many and need each one's corpus kept isolated. Multi-tenant out of the box, governed from a single panel.
construyendo.cardCTitulo: I'm adding RAG to my SaaS
construyendo.cardCDesc: You don't want to rebuild ingestion, scale, multi-tenancy and security. RAGfly gives it to you solved, consumed via MCP, REST or CLI.

problema.titulo1: The RAG that works with 1,000 documents
problema.descripcion: Almost all software will carry AI agents, and almost all of those agents need to talk to private documents: contracts, case files, regulations, records, manuals. That's where everyone hits the same wall: solving ingestion, vectorization, freshness, isolation between clients and auditing at scale becomes an infrastructure project in itself — expensive, slow and easy to get wrong.
problema.sin0: Assemble vector DB + parser + framework + your own security layer
problema.sin1: Build multi-client isolation by hand (and pray it doesn't leak)
problema.sin2: Upload the documents to a third-party cloud
problema.sin3: Lock yourself into a vector DB or LLM vendor
problema.sin4: Months of integration before the first result
problema.con0: Point at a directory; ingestion, vectorization and indexing are automatic
problema.con1: Multi-tenant out of the box: an isolated corpus per client, from the database up
problema.con2: The document never leaves your network; "in-flight" vectorization, optional Client LM
problema.con3: DB-agnostic and BYO: bring your own vector database and your own model
problema.con4: From zero to an agent answering with citations in under 30 minutes

queEs.descripcion: Point RAGfly at a directory of documents — thousands or tens of thousands, scanned ones included — and get a secure, multi-tenant, production-ready retrieval foundation. Retrieval at scale, isolation between clients and data governance come solved. You focus on your agent's logic; RAGfly is the ground it stands on.
queEs.bullet0: Pointing is your whole job — governing many clients doesn't cost you an engineering project
queEs.bullet1: Text, tables, layout and scans: PDF, Word, Excel, plain text
queEs.bullet2: Production-ready from the very first directory

combina.item0Desc: An isolated corpus per client, governed from a single panel with a Groups → Entities → Areas structure. Serve 20 clients from one platform without a single piece of data crossing from one to another. It's what cloud-first context engines don't solve: there, you build that isolation yourself.
combina.item1Titulo: The document never leaves your network
combina.item1Desc: "In-flight" vectorization, data encrypted at rest in the database and optional Client LM so indexing never leaves the internal network. Opens up the regulated clients — legal, healthcare, government — that others can't take. On the advanced plans, you also bring your own vector database and choose your models (BYO).
combina.item2Desc: No integration project. Ingestion, vectorization and indexing are automatic, at the scale of tens of thousands of files. Your only job is to point; the system handles the rest.
combina.capaTitulo: And the layer underneath — where the value lives
combina.capaDesc: Retrieval intelligence, not just storage: reranking, hybrid search, adaptive chunking, freshness, permission filtering and citation accuracy. It's what the agent can't do on its own — it can't see inside the corpus. And an action layer, not just a context layer: the agent invokes operations over sets of documents by prompt, not just retrieving chunks.
combina.tablaSin0: Assemble vector DB + parser + framework + your own security layer
combina.tablaCon0: Point at a directory; ingestion, vectorization and indexing are automatic
combina.tablaCon1: Multi-tenant out of the box: an isolated corpus per client, from the database up
combina.tablaSin2: Upload the documents to a third-party cloud
combina.tablaCon2: The document never leaves your network; "in-flight" vectorization, optional Client LM
combina.tablaSin3: Lock yourself into a vector DB or LLM vendor
combina.tablaCon3: DB-agnostic and BYO: bring your own vector database and your own model
combina.tablaSin4: Months of integration before the first result
combina.tablaCon4: From zero to an agent answering with citations in under 30 minutes

chat.eyebrow: Try it by talking to it
chat.descripcion: RAGfly has a chat that isn't for "chatting with your files" — it's for operating the platform by talking to it. Add a user, create a profile, change someone's area or request support, all from the same place. Need a token? Ask how and it takes you to the form. Change a user's area? It does it, or opens the screen for you.
chat.descripcion2: And yes, you can also query your documents and test retrieval before integrating anything. It's the fastest way to see the value in ten minutes — before writing a line of code.
chat.nota: A DX test, not the product: the chat uses every primitive, but the focus is on the infrastructure.
chat.demo0: add ana@client.com to the Finance area
chat.demo0r: User created. Inherits the "Finance Analyst" profile.
chat.demo1: summarize the contracts expiring this month
chat.demo1r: 3 contracts · cited to their source file
chat.demo2: give me an MCP token for my agent
chat.demo2r: Done. Opening the credentials form for you.

identidades.titulo1: You don't give your agent access to the documents.
identidades.descripcion: In RAGfly, everyone who queries is an identity with permissions. An identity can be a person (signs in with their email) or a profile: a reusable service identity, with a blind code and no personal data behind it.
identidades.p0: The agent comes in as "Finance Analyst" and sees exactly what that profile would see — its area, its level, its entity — with the same rules an employee would have. The RBAC that protects people protects agents just the same: the finance agent doesn't touch HR or another company in the holding.
identidades.p1: You define the profile once and assign it to any agent, today or next year. For a consultancy deploying agents for 20 clients, that's 20 isolated sets of profiles, governed from a single place. It's the answer to the number-one fear of anyone putting an agent into a regulated organization: that it leaks a document it shouldn't have seen.
identidades.tag2: Same RBAC for people and agents

modos.descripcion: Same interface. In the cloud you start in minutes. On Desktop, the data is never touched outside your network — not even to extract the content — which adds privacy and speed.
modos.cloudDesc: Start in minutes, with no infrastructure of your own. RAGfly provisions everything and you point at your directory.
modos.desktopDesc: The data never leaves your network, not even to extract content. Enables what the cloud can't: real filesystem paths.
modos.desktop0: Indexing never leaves the internal network
modos.desktop2: Drops retrieved documents into a folder ready for your local agent
modos.nota: Desktop doesn't replace your agent — Claude Code, whichever you use: it serves it better. It's the same RAGfly action layer, more powerful when it runs inside your network.

superficies.tituloAccent: Use it from wherever you work.
superficies.descripcion: All three surfaces, on every plan. The message is one of neutrality; the path we show first is MCP.
superficies.mcpDesc: Connect RAGfly as an MCP server and your agent converses with the documents without you building any UI. It's where we open the door.
superficies.restDesc: The universal production foundation. The base everything else sits on.
superficies.cliDesc: DX, operations and bulk ingestion. Day-to-day convenience.

construido.item0Desc: Postgres + pgvector (Supabase to get started) + embeddings. A dedicated vector DB stays as a scale option, and you can always bring your own (BYO vector database + BYO LLM).
construido.item1Desc: Each document's state lives in a relational database on Postgres: mixed SQL + vector queries inside your network, without a closed API in a third party's cloud.
construido.item2Desc: Text, tables, layout and messy scans (PDF, Word, Excel, plain text). Deliberate scope on text + OCR, no audio or video.
construido.item3Desc: Four levels — System, Group, Entity, User — where the lowest overrides the one above. The logic is configured with prompts that the engine compiles into behavior.

seguridad.descripcion: Protection is a design pillar, not a premium plan. Confidential documents stay under your organization's control.
seguridad.punto0: Documents aren't uploaded to a third-party cloud; indexing and vectorization happen "in-flight"
seguridad.punto1: Document data encrypted at rest in the database
seguridad.punto2: Structural filtering from the database up: every access goes through views that filter what each identity can see
seguridad.punto3: Full isolation between organizations: no group sees another's data
seguridad.punto4: Granular RBAC by area, role and profile — the same for people and agents
seguridad.punto5: Internal operation only through validated APIs: no surface for prompt injection
seguridad.punto6: Two-factor authentication and secure JWT tokens
seguridad.punto7: Full audit trail: every action logged with identity, date and module
seguridad.punto9: Optional Client LM: indexing of physical documents never leaves the internal network

planes.titulo: We charge per page processed. In pages and dollars, not credits.
planes.plan2F3: Multi-tenant: an isolated corpus per client
planes.plan4F0: Pages and entities to be defined
planes.nota: Storage at cost with a bounded multiplier that recovers infra administration — it's not a profit center. The margin lives in retrieval (the value), not in storage. Enterprise is a single inbound door: the deployment — large managed or on-prem/sovereign — is defined on the call.

porQue.item0Desc: Ingestion, scale, multi-tenancy and security come solved. You build the agent logic, not the infrastructure.
porQue.item1Titulo: Multi-tenant for those who serve many
porQue.item1Desc: Isolated clients from a single panel — what cloud-first vendors don't offer.
porQue.item2Titulo: The data never leaves your network
porQue.item2Desc: Client LM, in-flight vectorization, BYO vector database and BYO LLM. Opens up the clients others can't take.
porQue.item3Desc: DB-agnostic, compatible with Claude, GPT, Gemini and open-source models. Bring your DB and your model.
porQue.item4Titulo: From directory to production in minutes
porQue.item4Desc: No integration project, no developers needed to get going.
porQue.item5Titulo: The infrastructure nobody wants to rebuild
porQue.item5Desc: It's to document context infrastructure what Stripe is to payments.

cta.titulo: Building agents that need to talk to private documents?
cta.subtitulo: Start free and connect your first directory via MCP in minutes.

footer.tagline: Multi-tenant RAG infrastructure for your AI agents. The data never leaves your network.
